Name: Pattern Recognition - PE

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: in the successful subtree, PARALLEL(Authorize_Payment, Package_Items, Ship_Order) allows any order of these three activities. For example, Ship_Order can happen BEFORE Authorize_Payment or BEFORE Package_Items.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: From a domain perspective, you cannot ship an order before packaging the items, and you probably shouldnt ship it before authorizing payment. The model uses PARALLEL for Authorize_Payment, Package_Items, and Ship_Order. Parallel operator in process trees allows any interleaving (sequence) of the children.

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Also, there is another issue: The cancel_subtree includes Close_Order. The root sequence also includes Close_Order at the end. So the cancel path produces: Receive_Order, Check_Inventory, Cancel_Order, Close_Order, Close_Order. This implies Close_Order happens twice

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE

The duplicate Close_Order is more of a duplication issue, not exactly natural order of operations. I will focus on that, and perhaps mention the double Close_Order as well.
